content.text: The official TypeScript SDK for the Claude API is
(requires Node.js 20+ and TypeScript 4.9+), store your API key as the
environment variable, then make your first API call using

, and a messages array. The SDK provides full TypeScript type definitions, supports streaming responses with event handlers, async/await patterns, tool use with Zod schemas, structured JSON outputs, error handling with specific exception classes, automatic retries, timeout configuration, and platform integrations (Bedrock, Vertex AI, Foundry, AWS).

attrs.alt: TypeScript SDK request lifecycle: Your Code → SDK Core → HTTPS Transport → Anthropic API

content.text:
- Node.js 20 LTS or later (non-EOL)
- TypeScript 4.9 or later
- Bun 1.0+, Deno v1.28.0+, Cloudflare Workers, Vercel Edge Runtime

# Amazon Bedrock (new Mantle API)
npm install @anthropic-ai/bedrock-sdk

# Amazon Bedrock (legacy InvokeModel API)
npm install @anthropic-ai/bedrock-sdk

# Google Vertex AI
npm install @anthropic-ai/vertex-sdk

# Microsoft Foundry
npm install @anthropic-ai/foundry-sdk

# Claude Platform on AWS (beta)
npm install @anthropic-ai/aws-sdk
The SDK automatically reads the API key from the
in source code. The SDK also supports Workload Identity Federation for production environments.
Maximum tokens Claude can generate. This is a hard ceiling, not a target. Set high enough for your expected response
Claude's role, tone, and constraints before conversation starts (separate from messages list)
for all request params and response fields:
Documentation for each method, request param, and response field
is available in docstrings and will appear on hover in most modern editors.
—you must maintain conversation history and send it back with each turn:
, reducing perceived latency and enabling progressive rendering for chat interfaces, CLIs, and IDEs.
Count Tokens Before Making a Request

const message = await client.messages.create({ ... });

console.log(message.usage);  // { input_tokens: 25, output_tokens: 13 }

export {
  AnthropicError,
  APIError,
  APIConnectionError,
  APIConnectionTimeoutError,
  APIUserAbortError,
  NotFoundError,
  ConflictError,
  RateLimitError,
  BadRequestError,
  AuthenticationError,
  InternalServerError,
  PermissionDeniedError,
  UnprocessableEntityError,
} from './core/error';

Errors that are retried by default:
- Connection errors (network connectivity problem)
- 408 Request Timeout
- 409 Conflict
- 429 Rate Limit
- ≥500 Internal errors
By default, requests time out after
This results in a timeout up to
The SDK throws an error if a non-streaming request is expected to be above roughly
